plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that Module 1 established the innovator mindset and prepares a transition into the next stage of development.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From module completion to readiness for the next phase through a concise summary-and-bridge close.
plan_units.rationale: The source explicitly provides a closing summary and bridge. This unit should orient the learner, mark completion, and set up the transition without reteaching prior module content in detail.
source_refs: Module 1 Complete: The Mindset is Set.
Congratulations on completing Module 1.
Summary Slide & Bridge to Module 2
Closing video with talking head and text overlay.
plan_units.title: Module 1 Close: Mindset Complete

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain that innovation in this course context means intrapreneurship inside the hospital rather than innovation outside institutional constraints.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From a generic idea of innovation to a hospital-specific intrapreneurial mindset through explicit framing of internal system constraints.
plan_units.rationale: This is a core conceptual pillar and foundational to the module's mindset framing. It deserves standalone treatment because it shapes how all other ideas are interpreted.
source_refs: The Intrapreneurial Mandate
innovating inside the hospital
navigating existing politics, budgets, and legacy systems
plan_units.title: Innovation Is Inside-the-System Work

plan_units.learning_objective: Distinguish between kind clinical environments and wicked innovation environments in healthcare.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From confidence in familiar clinical pattern recognition to appreciation of ambiguous innovation work through contrast between kind and wicked environments.
plan_units.rationale: The contrast contains multiple explanatory beats and is central to mindset change, so it benefits from progressive disclosure rather than a single compressed treatment.
source_refs: Kind vs. Wicked Environments
Clinical medicine is often a kind environment
healthcare innovation is wicked, ambiguous, and variable
plan_units.title: Kind vs. Wicked: Why Innovation Feels Different

plan_units.learning_objective: Describe why clinical medicine is often experienced as a more rule-bounded, repetitive environment.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From confidence in familiar clinical pattern recognition to appreciation of ambiguous innovation work through contrast between kind and wicked environments.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial deepens the first side of the contrast so the later shift to wickedness feels earned and comprehensible.
source_refs: Clinical medicine is often a kind environment
repetitive patterns and clearer rules
plan_units.title: Clinical Medicine Often Rewards Patterned Practice

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain why healthcare innovation presents ambiguity, variability, and weaker pattern certainty than routine clinical work.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From confidence in familiar clinical pattern recognition to appreciation of ambiguous innovation work through contrast between kind and wicked environments.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial resolves the contrast by landing the learner in the actual innovation environment the course addresses.
source_refs: healthcare innovation is wicked, ambiguous, and variable
plan_units.title: Healthcare Innovation Requires Comfort With Ambiguity

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain why clinical leaders must use frameworks beyond their own specialty when solving innovation problems.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From specialty-bound problem solving to adaptive cross-functional thinking through permission to borrow nonclinical frameworks.
plan_units.rationale: This is a core pillar and important behavior change, but the source support is concise enough to keep it dense as a singleton.

The plan ends with the explicitly sourced bridge from Module 1 Mindset to Module 2 Leadership Identity.

cd_directive.creative_rationale: Choose visual-led to carry a momentum-rich bridge via on-camera presence and minimal overlays. This closing segment is about inspiration and identity shift, not dense instruction—perfect for a talking-head with concise text (“Module 1 Complete”) and a simple bridge graphic from Mindset to Identity. Visual emphasis lets Dr. Naik’s authority and the bridge metaphor do the heavy lifting, reducing cognitive load while reinforcing the pivot from diagnosing systems to embodying leadership. Adaptive density prevents clutter; source-grounded narration matches an on-camera clinician; balanced coupling times overlays to key beats (Opportunity Radar → leader readiness). The result preserves Module 1’s pillars and primes the learner to step into a defined leadership identity in Module 2.

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: A concise closing bridge that completes Module 1, reminds learners that mindset and opportunity recognition are only the starting point, and pivots them into Module 2's focus on leadership identity, communication, and influence within a complex health system. The plan preserves the explicit bridge text and graphic from source, clusters the higher-concept transition beats for progressive disclosure, and includes a production constraint unit so downstream teams do not assume unavailable motion assets.

output.additional_instructions: Experience profile: visual-led. Creative rationale: Choose visual-led to carry a momentum-rich bridge via on-camera presence and minimal overlays. This closing segment is about inspiration and identity shift, not dense instruction—perfect for a talking-head with concise text (“Module 1 Complete”) and a simple bridge graphic from Mindset to Identity. Visual emphasis lets Dr. Naik’s authority and the bridge metaphor do the heavy lifting, reducing cognitive load while reinforcing the pivot from diagnosing systems to embodying leadership. Adaptive density prevents clutter; source-grounded narration matches an on-camera clinician; balanced coupling times overlays to key beats (Opportunity Radar → leader readiness). The result preserves Module 1’s pillars and primes the learner to step into a defined leadership identity in Module 2. Slide mode proportions: creative=0.6, literal-text=0.15, literal-visual=0.25.
